title: QA: output.rs '(no new output)' path drops exit-code context for finished shells
description:
## Severity
Trivial
## Dimension
naming
## Location
src/tools/bash/output.rs:39-43
## Violation
When a finished shell produces no new output since the last poll, the user sees `Status: finished (exit code: 0)\n(no new output)` OR `Status: finished\n(no new output)` if the wait thread hasn't populated `exit_status` yet (see related race-condition issue). The path conflates "command produced no output" with "no new output since last drained" — a user who never polled before completion sees `(no new output)` and assumes the command was silent.

Quoted (output.rs:39-43):
```
            let result = if output.is_empty() {
                format!("Status: {status}\n(no new output)")
            } else {
                format!("Status: {status}\n\n{output}")
            };
```
## Mandated refactor
1. Distinguish in the manager: `OutputSlice { lines: Vec<String>, was_drained_before: bool }`.
2. Render `(command produced no output)` only when the lifetime-cumulative byte count is zero; render `(no new output since last poll)` otherwise.
3. For finished shells, always render the exit-code, never the bare "finished".
## Filed from QA review dispatch
